§6Color Applicator§f:
The §6Color Applicator §f(appliedenergistics2:color_applicator) is a §6tool §f that §6applies colours §f to §6ME Cables §f, §6ME Parts §f, and §6ME Facades§f. It is the §6primary way §f to customise the visual style of an AE2 network.

§6Production Path (tools/network_color_applicator.json)§f:
Pattern "ab / bc /  d" (an asymmetric 3x3 pattern):
- §ba§f: §6Formation Core §f(appliedenergistics2:material:43) — 1 (top left)
- §bb§f: §6Iron Ingot §f(ingotIron) — 2 (top middle + middle left)
- §cc§f: §6§e4k§r ME Storage Component §f(appliedenergistics2:material:36) — 1 (middle)
- §cd§f: §6ME Energy Cell §f(appliedenergistics2:energy_cell) — 1 (bottom right)
- Yields 1× Color Applicator

§6Function§f:
- §bCable colouring §f: right-click a §6ME Cable §f to apply a §bdye §f colour
- §bPaintball ammo §f: place a §6Paintball §f in the §6invenory §f slot to apply its colour
- §bInternal energy §f: stored in its own §6internal §f §b400,000 AE §f buffer
- §bPower cost §f: §b10 AE §f per right-click
- §bCharging §f: place in a §6Charger §f to refill its internal buffer
- §b16 colours §f: 16 §bdye §f colours plus 16 §blumen §f variants

§6Direct Descendants (TaskRetrieval graph)§f:
The jei-graph shows 0 items the Color Applicator is a component of, but it is referenced in 6 §6use sites §f across the mod pack. Common uses:
- §bNetwork aesthetics §f: colour §6cable segments §f to indicate §bchannel count §f or §6function §f
- §bCross-network separation §f: use distinct §6colours §f for distinct §6subnetworks §f
- §bPaintball crafting §f: the §6Paintballs §f are crafted via the §6Inscriber §f and §6Color Applicator §f

§6Practical Use§f:
- §eTip§f: a single §6Color Applicator §f can store §b1 paintball §f; use a §6Storage Cell §f to bulk-apply
- §eTip§f: use §6red §f for power, §6blue §f for data, and §6green §f for I/O to make a §6visual §f network map
- §cWarning§f: the Color Applicator §cdrains AE §f from its internal buffer; it is §cnot §f a §6FE/Tesla §f tool
- §cWarning§f: applying a colour to a §6Dense Cable §f will §ccolour §f the §bentire §f cable segment — there is no §6per-channel §f colouring
